__fallback__.headline: Offline tomato guidance
__fallback__.next_step: Retake a clear photo and confirm with a supporter before applying chemical treatment.
__fallback__.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
actions: Retake a close tomato leaf photo in even light if symptoms are unclear.
Separate heavily infected leaves or plants if safe for the crop.
Avoid broad chemical treatment based only on the offline result.
monitoring: Check spread again within 48 hours.
Inspect nearby tomato plants for similar symptoms.
prevention: Disinfect tools before moving between rows.
Reduce prolonged leaf wetness where possible.
escalate_if: Disease spreads rapidly across multiple rows.
Fruit, stem, or whole-plant collapse appears.
notes: This guidance is a crop-specific offline baseline and should be confirmed in the field.
__fallback__.verification_note: Draft treatment guidance. Expert agronomy review is required before pesticide use.

Tomato_healthy.headline: Leaf appears healthy
Tomato_healthy.next_step: No disease class was flagged. Continue routine scouting and only rescan if symptoms appear.
actions: Keep the plant under normal field management.
Scan again only if new lesions, spots, curling, or mites appear.
monitoring: Inspect lower and middle canopy during the next field round.
Watch for early lesions after humid or rainy periods.
prevention: Maintain clean tools and remove severely damaged leaves promptly.
Keep irrigation and nutrition balanced to reduce plant stress.
escalate_if: New spots or yellowing spread over several leaves.
Neighboring plants begin to show similar symptoms.
notes: Healthy predictions can still miss early field symptoms. Use routine scouting.
Tomato_healthy.verification_note: No pesticide treatment is recommended for a healthy result.

Tomato_Bacterial_spot.headline: Likely bacterial spot
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.next_step: Confirm in the field quickly. Prioritize sanitation and avoid overhead irrigation while you verify treatment options.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.active_ingredient: Fixed copper bactericide; copper plus mancozeb where locally registered.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.dosage: Use the registered label rate for the selected tomato bacterial spot product.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.ppe: Gloves and protective clothing; eye protection if spraying.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered product label PHI for tomato.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered product label REI.
actions: Remove badly infected leaves where practical.
Avoid working wet plants to reduce spread.
Keep tools clean between rows.
monitoring: Track whether new spots are appearing on young leaves.
Watch fruit and stem surfaces for expanding lesions.
prevention: Reduce splash spread from irrigation or heavy runoff.
Avoid moving from infected rows to clean rows without sanitation.
escalate_if: Fruit lesions become common.
Symptoms spread quickly after rainfall or irrigation.
notes: Copper-based programs may be used in some systems, but verify local suitability and label guidance first.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Tomato_Bacterial_spot.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.

Tomato_Early_blight.headline: Likely early blight
Tomato_Early_blight.next_step: Verify soon and reduce leaf wetness. Remove heavily damaged lower leaves first.
Tomato_Early_blight.active_ingredient: Protectant fungicide such as mancozeb or chlorothalonil; rotate with locally registered systemic fungicides when pressure is high.
Tomato_Early_blight.dosage: Use the locally registered label rate for tomato early blight control.
Tomato_Early_blight.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Tomato_Early_blight.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label PHI for tomato.
Tomato_Early_blight.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label REI.
actions: Prune or remove the most infected lower leaves if safe.
Keep foliage off wet soil where possible.
Improve airflow around the canopy.
monitoring: Check lower leaves and neighboring plants within 2 days.
Watch lesion expansion after humid nights or rain.
prevention: Avoid prolonged overhead irrigation.
Remove crop debris after harvest where practical.
escalate_if: Defoliation accelerates up the canopy.
Fruit lesions begin to appear.
notes: Field confirmation is still needed before choosing fungicide chemistry.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Tomato_Early_blight.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.

Tomato_Late_blight.headline: Possible late blight
Tomato_Late_blight.next_step: Treat as urgent. Isolate and verify immediately because spread can be rapid in cool, wet conditions.
Tomato_Late_blight.active_ingredient: Late blight fungicide program using locally registered oomycete-active products such as mandipropamid, dimethomorph, cymoxanil mixtures, or mefenoxam-based products, rotated with protectants.
Tomato_Late_blight.dosage: Use the locally registered label rate for the chosen late blight product and rotation program.
Tomato_Late_blight.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Tomato_Late_blight.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered late blight product PHI for tomato.
Tomato_Late_blight.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered late blight product REI.
actions: Separate severely affected plants or rows if feasible.
Avoid moving wet infected foliage through the field.
Prioritize expert confirmation the same day if symptoms are spreading.
monitoring: Inspect twice daily in cool, wet conditions.
Watch stems and fruit for dark water-soaked lesions.
prevention: Reduce prolonged leaf wetness and improve airflow immediately.
Clean tools and footwear before moving to unaffected blocks.
escalate_if: New infections appear across many plants within 24 to 48 hours.
Stem infections or fruit rot become visible.
notes: Late blight can move very quickly. Do not delay field verification.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Tomato_Late_blight.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.

Tomato_Leaf_Mold.headline: Likely leaf mold
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.next_step: Reduce humidity around the crop and confirm the diagnosis before treating.
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.active_ingredient: Protectant fungicide such as chlorothalonil or mancozeb where registered; rotate with labeled systemic fungicides if disease pressure remains high.
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.dosage: Use the locally registered label rate for tomato leaf mold control.
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label PHI for tomato.
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label REI.
actions: Remove badly affected leaves if this can be done safely.
Increase spacing or airflow where possible.
Avoid watering practices that keep leaves wet.
monitoring: Check undersides of leaves for mold growth.
Track spread through dense or shaded canopy areas.
prevention: Ventilate protected structures where applicable.
Avoid overcrowded canopy growth.
escalate_if: Defoliation increases quickly.
Large sections of the canopy show mold growth.
notes: Leaf mold risk rises in humid, poorly ventilated tomato canopies.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Tomato_Leaf_Mold.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.

Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.headline: Likely Septoria leaf spot
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.next_step: Verify soon and remove the most affected lower leaves to slow spread.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.active_ingredient: Protectant fungicide such as chlorothalonil or mancozeb; rotate fungicide groups under sustained pressure where locally registered.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.dosage: Use the locally registered label rate for tomato Septoria leaf spot control.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label PHI for tomato.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label REI.
actions: Prune badly affected lower leaves where practical.
Avoid splashing soil and debris onto foliage.
Keep field sanitation high.
monitoring: Check for fast spread from lower to upper canopy.
Inspect after wet weather events.
prevention: Reduce splash dispersal from irrigation and rain.
Remove infected debris after the crop cycle.
escalate_if: Defoliation becomes significant.
Symptoms move quickly through many plants.
notes: Septoria often starts low in the canopy and progresses upward.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.

Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.headline: Likely spider mite damage
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.next_step: Inspect leaf undersides and confirm mite presence before any treatment decision.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.active_ingredient: Miticide such as abamectin, bifenazate, spiromesifen, or another locally registered acaricide.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.dosage: Use the locally registered label rate for the selected miticide on tomato.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered miticide label PHI for tomato.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered miticide label REI.
actions: Check the underside of leaves for mites or fine webbing.
Remove the most heavily infested leaves if safe.
Reduce dusty conditions where possible.
monitoring: Inspect hot, dry field edges closely.
Track whether stippling spreads to new leaves.
prevention: Avoid plant stress from drought where possible.
Preserve beneficial insects when choosing interventions.
escalate_if: Webbing becomes visible across many leaves.
Mite pressure expands quickly during hot, dry weather.
notes: Confirm actual mite presence before treatment because nutrient stress can sometimes look similar.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.

Tomato__Target_Spot.headline: Likely target spot
Tomato__Target_Spot.next_step: Verify the lesions in the field and lower humidity or leaf wetness pressure while you assess severity.
Tomato__Target_Spot.active_ingredient: Protectant fungicide such as chlorothalonil or mancozeb, with locally registered systemic fungicides rotated where disease pressure is high.
Tomato__Target_Spot.dosage: Use the locally registered label rate for tomato target spot control.
Tomato__Target_Spot.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Tomato__Target_Spot.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label PHI for tomato.
Tomato__Target_Spot.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label REI.
actions: Remove the worst infected foliage where feasible.
Improve air movement through the canopy.
Avoid practices that keep foliage wet for long periods.
monitoring: Watch lesion growth on older leaves and fruit shoulders.
Check neighboring rows within 48 hours.
prevention: Keep the canopy open and sanitation consistent.
Remove plant debris after the season where practical.
escalate_if: Fruit lesions become common.
Leaf loss accelerates quickly.
notes: Target spot can resemble other foliar diseases. Field confirmation still matters.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Tomato__Target_Spot.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.

Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.headline: Possible Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.next_step: Verify quickly and focus on roguing infected plants and vector pressure management rather than curative treatment.
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.active_ingredient: No curative pesticide. Focus on whitefly vector management using a locally registered insecticide where appropriate.
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.dosage: Use the locally registered label rate for the selected whitefly-control product.
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered product label PHI if a vector-control spray is used.
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered product label REI if a vector-control spray is used.
actions: Mark or remove heavily affected plants if local practice supports it.
Inspect for whitefly pressure on nearby plants.
Avoid moving infested material into clean areas.
monitoring: Check new growth for curling and yellowing.
Track spread row by row, especially near field edges.
prevention: Manage whitefly vectors using integrated methods.
Keep weeds and alternate hosts under control where practical.
escalate_if: New plants show symptoms rapidly.
Whitefly pressure is visibly high.
notes: Virus problems are managed mainly through sanitation and vector control, not curative spray.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.

Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.headline: Possible Tomato mosaic virus
Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.next_step: Confirm early and focus on sanitation because infected plants usually do not recover.
Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.active_ingredient: No curative pesticide. Remove infected plants where feasible and rely on sanitation instead of spray treatment.
Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.dosage: No curative spray dose applies. If insect control is used for other pests, follow the locally registered label.
Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered product label PHI for any separate pest-control spray used.
Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered product label REI for any separate pest-control spray used.
actions: Avoid handling healthy plants after touching symptomatic plants without cleaning hands and tools.
Isolate or remove strongly affected plants where practical.
Do not use infected plant material for propagation.
monitoring: Check neighboring plants for mosaic, distortion, and stunting.
Monitor spread after handling or pruning operations.
prevention: Sanitize tools regularly.
Use clean seed or transplants where possible.
escalate_if: Symptoms spread after routine field work.
Large portions of the block show mosaic and stunting.
notes: Virus management depends heavily on hygiene and removing sources of infection.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
